title: How Long Do Dermal Filler Last？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: How Long Do Dermal Filler Last？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: How long does fill a lasts most practitioners will just say whatever the packet says and I have learned the hard way that this is absolutely not the case with everyone some people will break down filler too quickly so we actually all have hyaluronidase in our skin it's actually s…

segments.text: how long does fill a lasts most practitioners will just say whatever the packet says and I have learned the hard way that this is absolutely not the case with everyone some people will break down filler too quickly so we actually all have hyaluronidase in our skin it's actually something that our bodies make they will break down hyaluronic acid that you inject we just generally don't have it in very high concentrations in the dermis but some people have more of it because you will eventually treat someone who breaks down their photo really quickly and then you'll be a bit confused on the other side of the spectrum sometimes demo fillers lasts way longer than what it says on the packet and I'm personally experienced this and I've known it for years and which is you will see a patient come back and what you actually detect is that firstly it's there is a difference between the before and after it's just not the difference that you want so typically this is either a slight spread in the shape of the lip or the lips for example might project outwards slightly so

you've lost the projection of the pink part of it but the overall white part of the lip is a little bit fuller and you'll see this all over the internet when you look for it which is sometimes the whole of the top lip starts to project downward and you get this slight elongation of that part of the mouth and then you read what happens is practitioners retreat's you get the pink lip of the showing up again it looks like you've just had your lips done but that slowly diffuses and it hangs around I believe for years sometimes and if you're not aware of it you can eventually make people look down I think there is a subsection of people who get so long out of a dermal filler that we should occasionally be talking about reversing it before we repeat the next procedure not talking about every six months I'm thinking maybe every three years or so they may get a better result by getting rid of the old product this was actually shown in an MRI study that was talked about on a quite well-known YouTube channel if you searched for how long do more fillers last you'll find that I'm sure and it basically tells us that using an MRI scan what I really knew to be true which is you will see that they diffuse slightly and

they hang around in certain parts of the face way longer than the packet says I'm example to show you which is of a non-surgical rhinoplasty if someone the patient or trust did not have another procedure since you saw me she came back wondering if she needs to do repeat because she can see it it'll change an angle of her nose when I looked at her before picture in fact it was the after picture after her first procedure I could see that the filler had actually expanded and was actually fuller now when you felt what that area felt like it felt boggy this is what I believe happens when fillers overtime is in some people who don't break them down quickly or cleanly they spread slightly and they get they changed texture maybe the degree of moisture that they hold is like differently but they do not disappear and you can in a small subset of people get a degree a degrading result and effectively they actually look and like you've done a different treatment to what you're intended and this is just the nature of hyaluronic acids and why I think sometimes we should reverse occasionally for those people who get really long results and then the result changes so I hope that helps
